So for each bus, the assigned passengers are from index 'p' to 'p + k -1'.

If 'y' is in this range, then it is assigned to this bus.

So the check function can be implemented as:

public boolean canCatch(int y, int[] buses, int[] passengers, int capacity) {
    Arrays.sort(buses);
    Arrays.sort(passengers);
    int n = buses.length;
    int m = passengers.length;
    int pos = Arrays.binarySearch(passengers, y);
    if (pos >=0) return false; // y is already present
    pos = -pos -1; // insertion point

    int p = 0;
    for (int i=0; i < n; i++) {
        int d = buses[i];

So the check function can be implemented as:

public boolean canCatch(int y, int[] buses, int[] passengers, int capacity) {
    Arrays.sort(buses);
    Arrays.sort(passengers);
    int n = buses.length;
    int m = passengers.length;
    int pos = Arrays.binarySearch(passengers, y);
    if (pos >=0) return false; // y is already present
    pos = -pos -1; // insertion point

    int p = 0;
    for (int i=0; i < n; i++) {
        int d = buses[i];
        int count = 0;
        while (p < m && passengers[p] <= d) {
            p++;
        }
        int available = capacity;
        int assigned = 0;
        while (assigned < available && p - assigned < m +1) {
            // Check if the current passenger is 'y'
            if (passengers[p - assigned -1] == y) {
                return true;
            }
            assigned++;
        }
        // Also check if 'y' is within the current bus's capacity and time
        if (y <= d) {
            int needed = available - assigned;
            if (needed >0) {
                // 'y' can be assigned to this bus
                return true;
            }
        }
    }
    return false;
}
